title: Tamil Mixed Vegetable Kootu

ingredients.name: ash gourd, peeled and cubed
visual_checks: or yam, raw banana, or a mix of vegetables
ingredients.notes: or yam, raw banana, or a mix of vegetables

subs.name: bottle gourd or raw banana, cubed
toor dal or moong dal — moong gives a softer texture
3 tbsp coconut milk powder blended to a thick paste with a little water

steps.text: Pressure cook the chana dal with 1 cup water and turmeric for 3 whistles until soft but still holding shape — it should not be mushy. Let pressure release, drain excess water, and set aside.
Grind the coconut, cumin, black pepper, and green chilli with 4 tablespoons of water into a smooth, thick paste in a mixie. This paste provides both flavour and body to the kootu.
Cook the ash gourd and drumstick in 200 ml salted water with turmeric over medium heat until just tender, about 10 minutes. Do not discard the cooking liquid.

Add the cooked dal and the coconut paste to the vegetables and their cooking water. Stir well and simmer on medium for 6 minutes until the coconut paste is cooked, the raw smell is gone, and the mixture has thickened to a semi-dry consistency.
Heat oil in a small pan. Splutter mustard seeds, add urad dal and fry until golden, then add dried red chillies and curry leaves and fry 30 seconds. Pour the tempering over the kootu.
Check salt and toss gently to combine. The kootu should be thick and coat the vegetables — thicker than a kuzhambu but not as dry as a poriyal. Serve with rice as one of the main side dishes.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Follow the cooker manufacturer's fill, venting and opening instructions; never force the lid open under pressure.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: ash-gourd → bottle gourd or raw banana, cubed; chana-dal → toor dal or moong dal — moong gives a softer texture; coconut → 3 tbsp coconut milk powder blended to a thick paste with a little water
